payload.summary: Both amendments identify real correctness issues with the plan_hash backfill logic. Amendment fr-2-e54db26f correctly identifies that snapshot-based backfill can stamp the wrong plan version on legacy rows if persist_plan() has advanced the snapshot hash since the row was originally created. Amendment fr-2-371a0969 correctly identifies that the terminal-replay repair path (backfill_terminal_entry) only copies a caller-supplied hash and has no snapshot fallback, so legacy finalized rows replayed with plan_hash=None will remain without a plan_hash even after repair writes fill other fields.

votes.rationale: This is a genuine data integrity bug. The snapshot_plan_hash() call at lines 2917 and 3249 reads the *current* snapshot plan_hash, which persist_plan() can advance at any time (service.rs:370). A legacy row created under plan v1 that lacks a stored plan_hash will be stamped with v2 if persist_plan() ran between the original creation and the replay. This silently misattributes work to the wrong plan version. The fix request is sound: (1) add a regression test for a missing-hash legacy row that spans a plan evolution, and (2) avoid snapshot-based backfill unless the original plan version can be determined. The issue is not theoretical — any plan update between row creation and replay triggers it.

This is a real gap in the repair path. backfill_terminal_entry() (lines 2670-2689) only sets plan_hash when the caller provides one. The terminal-replay branch at lines 3218-3224 delegates entirely to this function, so if a legacy finalized row is replayed with plan_hash=None (which is the common legacy case), the repair will fill run_id, outcome_detail, and finished_at while leaving plan_hash as None. This is inconsistent with the non-terminal path at lines 3246-3249 which does attempt snapshot-based backfill (albeit with the issue identified in the other amendment). The requested regression test — preseeding a terminal row with plan_hash=None and replaying with caller plan_hash=None — would catch this asymmetry and ensure terminal repair paths handle plan_hash consistently.
